title: Pentacalia guadalupe
taxonomy: Pentacalia guadalupe is classified within the Asteraceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes. Pentacalia guadalupe is specifically a member of the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Eupatorieae. The genus Pentacalia is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is characterized by its distinctive leaf arrangement and flower structure. The classification of Pentacalia guadalupe reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Pentacalia guadalupe is a plant species native to Mexico, and while limited information is available on its medicinal uses, traditional practices in some regions suggest its potential as a natural remedy. Its leaves and stems may be used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Additionally, some studies have explored the plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. However, further research is necessary to fully understand and validate the medicinal uses of Pentacalia guadalupe. More studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.

intro: Pentacalia guadalupe, also known as Guadalupe pentacalia, is a plant species native to Mexico that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The health benefits of Pentacalia guadalupe include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pentacalia guadalupe is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
